arts.sum: Six Serie A fixtures are set to take place across the course of Sunday, beginning...

arts.ttl: Como against Hellas Verona for the overtake
arts.sum: Como have drawn six of their 12 previous matches in Serie A against Hellas Verona (W3 L3), including the most recent one, on 9 April 1989 – against none of the opponents they have faced at least 10 times in the competition have they recorded a higher percentage of draws (50%, as against Avellino and Ascoli). Como are unbeaten in their six home matches against Verona in Serie A (W2 D4), only against Bologna have they played more home games in the competition without ever losing (10).

arts.ttl: Como manager Fabregas prepares for Hellas Verona clash
arts.sum: Como head coach Cesc Fabregas addressed the media ahead of his team’s upcoming match against Hellas Verona, reflecting on their recent victory against Atalanta in Bergamo and discussing the challenges ahead. Fabregas began by acknowledging the strength of their next opponents: “Verona is a strong and experienced team accustomed to playing in Serie A.” He then praised his players for their performance in Bergamo, stating, “The boys put in a great performance against a top team. In the days since, we’ve tried to maintain high mental focus.” The Spanish coach emphasized the importance of the upcoming fixture: “Sunday won’t be The post Como manager Fabregas prepares for Hellas Verona clash appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Serie A | Como v Verona: Match Preview
arts.sum: The Giuseppe Sinigaglia Stadium in Como will host an intriguing Serie A matchup on Sunday as newly-promoted Como welcome Hellas Verona in a battle between two sides experiencing contrasting fortunes. Fresh off their first top-flight victory in over two decades, Como aim to build momentum against a Verona squad looking to halt a worrying slide. Cesc Fabregas’ Como side enter this fixture brimming with confidence after securing a dramatic 3-2 away victory over Atalanta last weekend – their first Serie A win since 2003.
